$comment: Per-brand config — lives at brand/<slug>/client-config.json (gitignored runtime data). Auto-sembrado por MC al crear el cliente (src/lib/data/client-config-seed.ts): incluye los crons marcados auto_onboarding:true en _system/cron-templates.json, enabled con su default_schedule. Safe to edit by hand. Tras crearlo, `workspace-sancho/scripts/create-client-crons.sh <slug>` crea los jobs reales.
name: My Client
$comment_publish: Brand-level default transport for cron output. 'slack' today; the publish registry (src/lib/publish/registry.ts) can add 'discord'/'telegram' later. Each cron may override it.
$comment_channels: Optional friendly-name → channel-id map. Reference these ids from crons.<x>.publish_channel, or put the channel id directly on the cron. Vacío al sembrar; rellenar al conectar Slack.
$comment_crons: Per-cron config. enabled + schedule + tz drive scheduling; publish_transport (optional, defaults to publish.default_transport → slack) y publish_channel deciden DÓNDE publica el cron vía POST /api/integrations/publish. Los 3 primeros son auto_onboarding (sembrados default-on); los demás son opt-in (poné enabled:true para activarlos). Nunca hardcodees un channel id en un skill/prompt — viene de acá.
